meta.title: Jonathan Brownstein — Senior Actuarial Analyst / Actuary
meta.description: Senior analytics professional with 15 years of experience inside regulated financial-services and global industrial organizations, the last seven inside the Data Analytics Office of a global reinsurer where I work direct

hero.description: Senior analytics professional with 15 years of experience inside regulated financial-services and global industrial organizations, the last seven inside the Data Analytics Office of a global reinsurer where I work directly with actuarial leadership, finance, and the C-suite. I build the predictive models, forecasting platforms, KPI dashboards, and executive reporting that drive period-close reviews and the next round of decisions. Day-to-day work covers ARIMA-based reserve-adjacent forecasting, classification machine learning rebuilds of regulated reporting, period-over-period valuation movement analysis, and outlier-detection frameworks across platforms processing more than a trillion records. Hands-on across SQL, R, Python, advanced Excel and VBA, Power BI, Tableau, SSRS, Databricks lakehouse, and Delta Lake. MS in Economics with an Applied Econometrics certificate, BS in Economics, and FLMI from LOMA.

highlights.label: Predictive analytics and classification machine learning

experience.title: Professional Experience
experience.intro: Experience tailored for the Senior Actuarial Analyst / Actuary opportunity.

items.title: Senior Data Analyst III
items.context: Senior reinsurance analytics owner inside the Data Analytics Office
items.intro: Senior individual contributor and technical lead inside the Data Analytics Office of a global reinsurer. Own the predictive models, forecasting platforms, KPI dashboards, and executive reporting that actuarial leadership, finance, valuation, and risk rely on, and lead analysts and engineers on multiple concurrent enterprise initiatives.

subsections.title: Forecasting, Reserving-Adjacent Analytics, and Predictive Modeling
bullets: Designed and deployed an automated ARIMA-based time-series forecasting platform for Net Amount at Risk on a scheduled production pipeline. Surfaces anomalies and trend movement before period close on a quarterly cadence, replacing manual reconciliation cycles with a governed, audit-ready forecasting view.

Built the firm's first valuation extract comparison architecture, a repeatable framework for period-over-period movement analysis across time periods and source systems. Other teams have since extended it for their own variance, reserve-movement, and trend work.
Led the classification machine learning rebuild of a regulated reporting workflow, materially improving accuracy, consistency, and audit readiness. The company's former CEO recognized the result.

Built statistical outlier and anomaly-detection frameworks using probability density function (PDF) methods across platforms processing more than a trillion records, catching attribute drift before it reaches downstream actuarial and finance reports.

subsections.title: Reporting, Dashboards, and Action Monitors
bullets: Build and maintain the Power BI, Tableau, and SSRS dashboards actuarial leadership, finance, and the C-suite use to read KPIs, variances, and movements. Translating model output and complex multi-source data into a clean executive narrative is the part of the role leadership leans on most.

Set engineering and analytics standards on metadata, lineage, data quality, and audit controls across the Data Analytics Office, and architected the Field Inventory Report that became the data-lineage reference standard for the Strategic Data Warehouse.
Designed action-monitor reporting that tracks profitability and operational KPIs against target ranges, with exception flags routed to the actuaries and business leaders who own the next decision.

subsections.title: Cross-Functional Partnership and Technical Mentorship
bullets: Partner directly with actuarial, valuation, finance, risk, IT, BI, and compliance to turn business questions into governed analytics, predictive models, and forecasting deliverables on a deadline. Communicate results in plain language to mixed actuarial and non-actuarial audiences, including senior leadership.

Lead project teams of analysts and engineers across concurrent enterprise initiatives, running architecture and design reviews, code reviews, and delivery checkpoints, and mentoring junior staff on SQL, Python, R, and Databricks on a recurring cadence.
Communicate potential analytical and data-quality issues to actuarial and finance leadership promptly, with concrete options for remediation.

items.title: Assistant Business Controller
items.organization: Chicago Pneumatic Company LLC (Atlas Copco Group)
items.context: FP&A, reporting, and analytics partner inside a global industrial manufacturer
bullets: Owned FP&A and business-partnering support for finance, sales, operations, marketing, HR, and regional leadership inside a global industrial manufacturer, feeding annual planning, monthly forecasting, and the executive reporting that drove the next round of decisions.
Designed automated KPI dashboards and reporting frameworks for sales, operations, and finance leadership, replacing manual reconciliation work and shortening month-end close commentary cycles.

Led cross-functional reporting modernization that improved data accessibility, consistency, and operational efficiency across finance and operations, and established the data-governance and QA practices the team continued to build on.

Served as the working bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams on executive priorities, and managed outside vendors and consultants on analytics, reporting, and finance-systems projects end to end.

groups.title: Languages and Tools: SQL, R, Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, statsmodels), advanced Excel and VBA
items: Reporting and Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS
Analytics and Modeling: ARIMA and time-series forecasting, classification machine learning, statistical outlier and anomaly detection, data mining, applied econometrics
Data Platforms: Databricks lakehouse, Delta Lake, Spark, cloud data warehousing (Snowflake-equivalent pattern), relational databases, ELT and ETL pipelines, database management
